headline: Philip Morris beats on earnings but stock edges lower on weak guidance
summary: Investing.com -- Philip Morris International Inc. (NYSE: PM) reported second-quarter earnings and revenue that topped consensus expectations on Wednesday, however, shares have edged lower after the company's Q3 earnings guidance missed the consensus estimate.

content.title: Philip Morris beats on earnings but stock edges lower on weak guidance
content.summary: Investing.com -- Philip Morris International Inc. (NYSE: PM) reported second-quarter earnings and revenue that topped consensus expectations on Wednesday, however, shares have edged lower after the company's Q3 earnings guidance missed the consensus estimate.

headline: Philip Morris (NYSE:PM) Surprises With Q2 CY2026 Sales
summary: Tobacco company Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026, with sales up 10.4% year on year to $11.19 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.80 per share was 9% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Philip Morris (NYSE:PM) Surprises With Q2 CY2026 Sales
content.summary: Tobacco company Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026, with sales up 10.4% year on year to $11.19 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.80 per share was 9% below analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Oil prices rise another 3%, while Wall Street drifts in mixed trading
summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Wednesday, while oil prices climb another 3% as fighting continues in the war with Iran.  The majority of stocks in the S&P 500 rose after more companies reported strong profits for the spring.  Expectations are high for the reports, and companies will need to match them after their stock prices already neared records on anticipation for them.

content.title: Oil prices rise another 3%, while Wall Street drifts in mixed trading
content.summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting in mixed trading Wednesday, while oil prices climb another 3% as fighting continues in the war with Iran. Expectations are high for the reports, and companies will need to match them after their stock prices already neared records on anticipation for them.
